name: Ethel's Grill
tagline: Tiny Kalihi gem where the garlic ahi and oxtail soup are worth the early wake-up.
mustOrder: Garlic ahi steak — seared rare with garlic butter, the signature dish
Oxtail soup — rich, fall-off-the-bone, with mustard cabbage
Shoyu pork — tender braised pork belly in sweet shoyu
parking: Small lot in front of the strip mall. Gets packed during lunch — arrive by 10:30am.
talkStory: Run by the same family for decades in an unassuming Kalihi strip mall, Ethel's is where construction workers and off-duty chefs eat side by side at the counter. The garlic ahi — seared rare and swimming in garlic butter — is legitimately one of the best things you can eat on Oahu for under $15. Featured on Guy Fieri's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives, which caused lines but didn't change the food. Lunch is the move — hot items start rolling at 10am and sell out fast.
